name: py-spy (Python Sampling Profiler)

auth.notes: Requires root or ptrace permissions to attach to process

performance.notes: Sampling profiler; <1% overhead; safe for production

agent_readiness.notes: Sampling profiler for Python processes without modification. py-spy top --pid PID (live top-like view). py-spy record -o profile.svg --pid PID (flamegraph). py-spy dump --pid PID (stack traces). No code modification required. Works on running production processes. Rust-based — minimal overhead. Supports CPython 2.7+, 3.x, PyPy. Docker: py-spy top --pid PID inside container. Flamegraph SVG output opens in browser. The go-to Python production profiler.

gotchas: Requires root or elevated permissions to attach to process
Sampling profiler — misses very short-lived functions
GIL means CPU time != wall time for I/O-bound Python
best_when: Profiling production Python processes without restart
CPU flamegraph generation for Python performance debugging
Identifying hot loops in running Python services
avoid_when: AsyncIO profiling (async frames may show incorrectly)
summary: py-spy profiles Python processes without code modification or restart. py-spy top --pid PID for live CPU view. py-spy record -o profile.svg for flamegraph. Rust-based with <1% overhead — safe for production. Attach to any running CPython process. Docker-compatible. The standard Python production profiler for CPU bottleneck investigation.
